P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
William A. O'Malley 
District Attorney 
P.O. Box 670 
Martinez, CA 
94553 
Dear Mr. O'Malley: 
Administratian 
•• 
Executive/Legal 
322·5660 
322·5901 
Enfarcement 
322-6441 
December 24, 1980, 
Statements of Ecanamic IntEH'est 
322-6444 
This. is in response to your inquiry about the reporting of 
gifts under Chapter 7 of the Political Reform Act. 
The following 
advice is provided pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b). 
The facts as I understand them from your letter are as follows. 
You are the District Attorney of Contra Costa County. 
Your 
son works for World Airways and you are consequently able to 
obtain standby airline tickets at no cost. 
During the year, you 
have traveled to London, England and to Boston using such tickets 
obtained for you by your son. 
You have asked wh~ther you must 
report these tickets on your Statement of Economic Interests. 
As District Attorney you are required to file a Statement 
each year disclosing all investments,interests in real property 
and income, including gifts of $25 or more in value. 
See 
Government Code Sections 87200, et~. However, the tickets you 
used to fly to Boston and London-Were obtained by your son for 
you, and were made available to him as a benefit of his 
employment. 
Consequently, to the extent that the tickets are 
gifts to you, they can be seen as gifts wh ich were made by your 
son, and the Political Reform Act provides that the term "gift" 
does not include gifts from an individual's child. 
Government 
Code Section 82028(b)(3). 
If in the future the tickets become 
available to you directly, i.e., without the necessity of your 
son obtaining them for you, you should contact us again. 
Under 
such altered circumstances the tickets may become reportable 
gifts from World Airways. 
STC:kp 
Sincerely, 
Sarah Cameron 
Deputy Chief for 
Conflict of Interest 
Legal Division 


istrict Attorney 
Court House, Fourth Floor 
P.O. BOl( 670 
Martinez, California 94553 
(415) 372 ·4500 
Contra 
Costa 
County 
November 14, 1 980 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Attention: 
Sarah Cameron, Division Chief 
Conflict of Interest 
Dear Miss Cameron: 
My son is employed by World Airways. 
Parents of 
employees of World may obtain standby airline tickets 
on World planes at no cost. 
My son obtained a ticket for me which I used to go 
to London and back. 
He also obtained another ticket 
which I used to travel to Boston and back. 
Is it necessary to claim the value of these tickets 
on my disclosure statement? 
Thank you for your assistance. 
Very truly yours~ 
District Attorney 
WAO:cas 
William A. 
District 
Gary 
Chief

sections.question: You are the District Attorney of Contra Costa County. Your son works for World Airways and you are consequently able to obtain standby airline tickets at no cost. During the year, you have traveled to London, England and to Boston using such tickets obtained for you by your son. You have asked whether you must report these tickets on your Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion: As District Attorney you are required to file a Statement each year disclosing all investments, interests in real property and income, including gifts of $25 or more in value. However, the tickets you used to fly to Boston and London were obtained by your son for you, and were made available to him as a benefit of his employment. Consequently, to the extent that the tickets are gifts to you, they can be seen as gifts which were made by your son, and the Political Reform Act provides that the term "gift" does not include gifts from an individual's child. Government Code Section 82028(b)(3). If in the future the tickets become available to you directly, i.e., without the necessity of your son obtaining them for you, you should contact us again. Under such altered circumstances the tickets may become reportable gifts from World Airways.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a District Attorney report the value of standby airline tickets obtained at no cost through his son's employment at World Airways on his Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The standby airline tickets obtained through the District Attorney's son are not reportable because they are gifts from his child, which are exempt under Government Code Section 82028(b)(3). However, if the tickets become directly available to him without his son's involvement, they would become reportable as gifts from World Airways.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Complete advice letter with both incoming request and FPPC response. Question and conclusion sections identified from document text. Document contains two parts: incoming letter from District Attorney and FPPC response letter from Deputy Chief Sarah Cameron.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You are the District Attorney of Contra Costa County. Your son works for World Airways and you are consequently able to obtain standby airline tickets at no cost. During the year, you have traveled to London, England and to Boston using such tickets obtained for you by your son. You have asked whether you must report these tickets on your Statement of Economic Interests.

CONCLUSION: As District Attorney you are required to file a Statement each year disclosing all investments, interests in real property and income, including gifts of $25 or more in value. However, the tickets you used to fly to Boston and London were obtained by your son for you, and were made available to him as a benefit of his employment. Consequently, to the extent that the tickets are gifts to you, they can be seen as gifts which were made by your son, and the Political Reform Act provides that the term "gift" does not include gifts from an individual's child. Government Code Section 82028(b)(3). If in the future the tickets become available to you directly, i.e., without the necessity of your son obtaining them for you, you should contact us again. Under such altered circumstances the tickets may become reportable gifts from World Airways.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises a District Attorney that standby airline tickets obtained at no cost through his son's employment at World Airways are not reportable gifts on his Statement of Economic Interests because they are gifts from his child, which are exempt under law.
